title: gladiolus segetum var. guepinii
intro: Gladiolus segetum var. guepinii, also known as the corn flag lily or wild gladiolus, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the iris family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. Gladiolus segetum var. guepinii has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
